lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Six forty-eight. The overhead fluorescents are at full. They have been at full since before the night shift started and they will be at full long after this one ends. The ER does not dim for anyone. That is not a complaint. It is the contract.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Maeve arrives three minutes early. Signs in at the nurses' station, sets her bag under the counter, pulls on gloves without checking the size. Her hands are steady.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Board's full. Bay two turnaround was slow overnight — chest pain, still waiting on echo. Harte's office wants a tally of last week's diverts by nine.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Maeve moves to the board. She reads through it left to right — chief complaint first, then duration, then which resident is covering. She adds nothing. She changes nothing. Whatever weight she is carrying, she carries it with both hands on the marker.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dana is already on the floor. Came in ten minutes before the shift started and did not sign the early-arrival log. Maeve sees her at the far end of the bay, rechecking a supply cart with the methodical quiet that reads as calm and might not be.

lines.speaker: DANA
lines.text: Trauma one's stocked. Bay four needed saline — I put in a request before it could run short.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: One word. The right word. Dana returns to the cart. Maeve returns to the board. The PA calls a name no one here recognises. Bay doors open and close. The ER absorbs the start of the shift the same way it absorbs every shift — without adjustment, without acknowledgment, without any interest in what any of these people did with their nights before they came back.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Maeve picks up a chart. Her hands are steady. She made sure of that — not at six forty-eight, not on the drive in, but earlier, in the car park, before the building could see her. She knows the difference between being steady and looking steady. Inside an ER, looking is almost always enough.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Reza's on at seven-thirty. Rounding now.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Same lights. Same noise underneath. The shift begins.
